observation.summary: Native config overlay docs described T-CFG decisions, security gates, store-channel policy placement, runtime secret evidence, and unsafe bypass policy as manually edited markdown while adjacent evidence artifacts already existed.

hypothesis.summary: A native-config-overlay manifest can generate the root and six manual detail docs, link adjacent full-access/runtime-secret/native-config-MCP evidence, and verify policy/workdir/IR/session source anchors without changing runtime behavior.

execution.summary: Generate the native config overlay index and detail pages from SSOT fragments while leaving full-access harness evidence under its existing dedicated generator.

evaluation.summary: The verifier rejects generated doc drift, missing source anchors, missing fragments, missing workflow wiring, and missing coverage registration.

retrospective.summary: The T-CFG native config overlay docs become generated evidence and reduce the security manual hotspot while preserving adjacent evidence ownership.

evidence.proves: security native config overlay docs are generated and source anchors still exist
policy, native-config versioning, tool blocking, event ingestion, and daemon policy defaults still match the documented boundary
public CI uploads security native config overlay evidence
